title: Concurrent installs break CPU-pod wrapper: SSE stall + false 'no folder' failure
description:
BUG (found 2026-07-03 during MPI-148 live verify on v0.11.0-cpu). Two related failures when installing models on a CPU download Pod, both rooted in the wrapper handling concurrent installs on shared state. NOT a v0.11.0 regression (wrapper 0.2.23 unchanged); single sequential install works (cpu smoke passed).

--- SYMPTOM 1: SSE stall / UI freeze ---
Starting 3 installs at once (LTX 40GB + Wan 5B 17GB + PiD 17GB) freezes the progress UI (bars stop, Wan+PiD at aria2's ~3MB preallocation floor, MODELS 0/9) though downloads still trickle (server reconcile settles completions off the volume). Cause: app POSTs installs concurrently; wrapper spawns each as an independent asyncio.create_task(_run_install) (wrapper.py:1563) - no cross-install serialization. On the tiny CPU pod, ~74GB across many aria2c '-x16 -s16' streams (48+ sockets) starves the wrapper -> it can't answer GET /wrapper/events/stream -> RunPod proxy returns HTTP error -> app fires reason 'bad-response' (remoteModels.js:556, guard '!res.ok || !res.body') then 'silent-stall' (downloadManager.js:884). Reconnect loops ~90s but the wrapper stays starved so recovery never sticks - the MPI-136 stall-belt was built for ONE zombie socket, not sustained N-way starvation. NOTE: 'bad-response' also seen on a SINGLE Wan install (8-11 deps) - it just recovers faster with fewer deps, so the flakiness is not purely 3-way; any load can trip it on the CPU pod.

--- SYMPTOM 2: false 'archive produced no folder' error ---
Installing Wan 5B (its custom-node dep) popped a Download-Failed dialog: 'Failed to download Wan 2.2 5B: archive produced no folder' - BUT the folder actually appeared moments later (user: 'as I was writing this, it downloaded it'). So it is a FALSE failure. Cause: the node-install path (wrapper.py:1414-1424) detects the extracted dir by a before/after set-diff of CUSTOM_NODES_DIR; if new_dirs is empty it raises 'archive produced no folder'. That diff is fragile: (a) a concurrent install writing into CUSTOM_NODES_DIR between the 'before' snapshot (1417) and the 'after' listdir (1421) can zero the diff, and (b) if the target dir already existed (prior partial) the diff finds nothing new even though files ARE present. There is NO fallback: it never checks whether node_dir itself now exists on disk before declaring failure (1447-1454 just reports install_failed). So a succeeded extract surfaces a ui:error GitHub-report dialog.

REPRO 1: connect a CPU pod, start LTX + Wan 5B + PiD together -> app.log 'SSE closed (bad-response)' x5 then 'silent-stall' loop; bars freeze. REPRO 2: install a model with a custom-node dep on a busy CPU pod -> intermittent 'archive produced no folder' dialog that is false (folder appears).

FIX OPTIONS:
A. App-side: serialize install POSTs (queue one model at a time). Smallest, no wrapper rebuild, kills BOTH symptoms' concurrency trigger. Preferred first move.
B. Wrapper (needs image rebuild + republish): (1) an asyncio.Lock/semaphore around _run_install to cap concurrent aria2; (2) node-install: replace the before/after set-diff with a deterministic detection (extract to a temp dir then move, OR after raise check 'if os.path.isdir(node_dir): treat as success') so a succeeded extract never reports a false failure.
C. Lower aria2 -x/-s under concurrency; raise the silent-stall tolerance on CPU pods.
Prefer A first (app-only, testable without rebuild); then B(2) for the false-failure fallback (cheap, high-value - stops a false GitHub-report dialog). Verify: 3-at-once install on a CPU pod keeps SSE alive + bars moving; no false 'no folder' dialog.

SEVERITY: medium - no data loss (downloads complete), but both read as hard failures (freeze / error dialog) and users will cancel/retry or file a bogus GitHub report.
BRANCH: RunPod (app serialize) + mpi-ci (wrapper lock + node-detect fallback).
